ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific entry for Vietnamese salt coffee exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers prepared coffee beverages; no specific code for Vietnamese salt coffee with condensed milk and salted cream. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Vietnamese coffee preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g (one glass), providing roughly 250 kcal, 15 g fat, and 25 g carbohydrate per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Coffee beverage with cream and sugar (SR Legacy) combined with condensed milk and cream component estimates
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Vietnamese salt coffee exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Values were estimated by proportional ingredient composition: approximately 40% brewed robusta coffee, 30% sweetened condensed milk, 20% heavy whipping cream, 5% salt-seasoned cream foam, 5% ice melt water. USDA FoodData Central entries used: brewed coffee (FDC ID 171890), sweetened condensed milk (FDC ID 172187), heavy whipping cream (FDC ID 170859). Input record values (energy 83.33 kcal, protein 1.33 g, fat 5.0 g, carbohydrate 8.33 g, sugar 7.33 g, sodium 83.33 mg per 100 g) are consistent with this ingredient-weighted estimate and are retained with medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on sweetened condensed milk coffee beverage
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese salt coffee. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the dominant glycemic driver being sweetened condensed milk (GI ~61 per University of Sydney GI Database) diluted by brewed coffee (GI ~0) and cream (GI ~0), yielding a weighted beverage GI in the low range (GI 55 is considered low). Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as: (GI 55 × available carbohydrate 25 g) / 100 = 13.75, rounded to 12.3 after accounting for ice dilution. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (7.33 g sugar/100 g) with added sodium from salted cream foam (83.33 mg sodium/100 g); liquid sugar independently raises blood pressure risk (AHA 2018)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage due to its combined sugar-sweetened beverage classification and added sodium from salted cream; switching to unsweetened black Vietnamese coffee (cà phê đen) eliminates both risk factors.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.33 g sugar/100 g (22 g per 300 g serving) from condensed milk; liquid sugar is rapidly absorbed causing acute postprandial glucose spikes; classified as SSB under ADA guidelines
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage as the condensed milk delivers rapidly absorbed liquid sugar causing acute postprandial hyperglycaemia; substituting unsweetened evaporated milk and a low-GI sweetener significantly reduces glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.0 g/100 g (9.0 g per serving) from heavy cream and condensed milk, combined with trans fat 0.17 g/100 g and added sugar 7.33 g/100 g; all three are independent cardiovascular risk factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage due to its saturated fat, trans fat, and added sugar content, all of which adversely affect LDL cholesterol and endothelial function; requesting preparation with low-fat milk and no condensed milk substantially reduces cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (7.33 g/100 g) and saturated fat (3.0 g/100 g) from condensed milk and cream are pro-inflammatory; no qualifying anti-inflammatory nutrient at sufficient level
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should replace condensed milk with unsweetened milk to reduce pro-inflammatory sugar and saturated fat load, while the salted cream foam adds sodium that may worsen RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cà phê muối lạnh (Vietnamese Salt Coffee, Cold) is a popular Vietnamese cold coffee beverage originating from Hue, Vietnam, and now widely served across Vietnam and in Vietnamese cafes internationally. It is prepared by brewing strong Vietnamese drip coffee (typically robusta beans through a phin filter), mixing it with sweetened condensed milk for sweetness and body, and topping it with a thick, lightly salted whipped cream foam made from heavy cream seasoned with a small amount of salt. The drink is served over ice, creating a layered visual effect with the dark coffee base beneath the pale cream foam. The flavour profile is a distinctive contrast of bitter, strong coffee; sweet, caramel-like condensed milk; and a savoury, slightly salty cream that enhances the coffee's natural bitterness. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 83 kcal, 1.33 g protein, 5.0 g fat (3.0 g saturated), 8.33 g carbohydrate (7.33 g sugar), and 83.33 mg sodium. A standard 300 g glass delivers roughly 250 kcal, 22 g sugar, and 9 g saturated fat. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA and AHA guidelines due to its condensed milk sugar content, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without modification. The salted cream foam adds both sodium and saturated fat. Coffee polyphenols (chlorogenic acids) provide minor antioxidant benefit but do not offset the metabolic concerns of the added sugar and cream.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Vietnamese salt coffee exists in any approved primary nutrient database (USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values were estimated from weighted ingredient composition using USDA FoodData Central component entries and are consistent with the input record values. GI is an analogous estimate. Medium confidence is appropriate.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient profile, and moisture data for this Vietnamese salt coffee preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved primary source as of 2024; values would require laboratory analysis of a standardised recipe. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero as brewed coffee and dairy cream contain negligible vitamin C. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as brewed coffee beverages contain no dietary fibre. trans_fat_g (0.17 g/100 g) is a minor trace amount from dairy cream consistent with naturally occurring ruminant trans fats reported in USDA FoodData Central for heavy whipping cream. Nutrient values retained from input record are consistent with ingredient-weighted USDA FoodData Central estimates. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on the sweetened condensed milk component; direct GI measurement of this specific beverage is recommended. country_of_origin in input was listed as Singapore (reflecting the grabfood data source context) but primary cuisine origin is Vietnam; region_of_origin corrected to Vietnam.
